frontier_justification: Structure-based drug design via deep learning (DiffDock, AlphaFold3) is a 2025-2026 frontier in computational chemistry. The ability to generate drug-like molecules that bind specific protein pockets with high affinity is actively transforming pharmaceutical discovery pipelines.

papers.title: Neural Message Passing for Quantum Chemistry
papers.key_contribution: MPNN: unified message passing framework for molecular property prediction; state-of-the-art on QM9 quantum chemical properties.

idea_genome.problem_genome: No unified framework for applying deep learning to molecular graphs; diverse architectures prevent systematic comparison.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Abstract message passing: node features updated by aggregating transformed neighbor messages; readout function pools atom features to molecular properties.
idea_genome.observation_genome: MPNN achieves state-of-the-art on 11 of 13 QM9 targets; unifies ConvMol, Weave, and other graph NNs under single framework.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Predicts molecular properties from 2D graphs; cannot model 3D structure; no binding affinity prediction; no protein-ligand interaction.

papers.title: Atomic Convolutional Networks for Predicting Protein-Ligand Binding Affinity
papers.key_contribution: AtomConv: applies MPNN-style graph convolutions to 3D protein-ligand complexes; predicts binding affinity from co-crystal structures.

idea_genome.problem_genome: MPNN predicts isolated molecular properties; drug discovery requires protein-ligand binding affinity from 3D complex structure.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Atomic convolution over protein-ligand complex: atom features updated by distance-weighted 3D neighbor messages; separate encoders for protein and ligand atoms.
idea_genome.observation_genome: AtomConv matches or exceeds Random Forest baselines on PDBbind binding affinity prediction; demonstrates DL applicability to structure-based design.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Requires co-crystal structure (rarely available for new targets); cannot predict docked pose; limited to small molecule ligands.

papers.title: EquiBind: Geometric Deep Learning for Drug Binding Structure Prediction
papers.key_contribution: EquiBind: predicts protein-ligand binding pose directly without exhaustive docking search; SE(3)-equivariant GNN for both protein and ligand.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Classical docking (AutoDock) requires exhaustive conformational search; AtomConv cannot predict binding pose, only score given poses.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: SE(3)-equivariant GNN processes protein and ligand separately; cross-attention predicts binding geometry; rigid-body alignment to protein pocket.
idea_genome.observation_genome: 1200× faster than traditional docking; competitive RMSD on PDBbind test set; enables screening large compound libraries.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Ligand flexibility poorly handled (rigid docking assumption); lower accuracy than physics-based methods on difficult targets; no uncertainty quantification.

papers.title: DiffDock: Diffusion Steps, Twists, and Turns for Molecular Docking
papers.key_contribution: DiffDock: diffusion over SE(3) ligand pose (translation, rotation, torsion angles); generates diverse binding pose hypotheses; surpasses all prior blind docking methods.

idea_genome.problem_genome: EquiBind produces single rigid-body pose; real binding is flexible; need multi-modal pose distribution over all degrees of freedom.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Generative diffusion over 3D pose space: separate diffusion processes for translation (R³), rotation (SO(3)), and torsion angles; confidence ranking of generated poses.
idea_genome.observation_genome: DiffDock achieves 38% top-1 success rate on PDBbind vs 23% for Glide (commercial); generates diverse pose hypotheses; state-of-the-art blind docking.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Slow sampling (multiple denoising steps); still requires known protein structure; cannot generate novel ligands; no co-design of protein and ligand.

papers.title: Accurate structure prediction of biomolecular interactions with AlphaFold 3
papers.key_contribution: AlphaFold3: unified diffusion-based model for structure prediction of proteins, DNA, RNA, small molecules, and their complexes; covers all biomolecular interaction types.

idea_genome.problem_genome: DiffDock requires separate protein structure prediction (AlphaFold2) and ligand docking; no unified model for all biomolecular complex types.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Diffusion over 3D atomic coordinates for all molecule types; Pairformer trunk encodes inter-chain relationships; trained on PDB structures of all complex types.
idea_genome.observation_genome: AlphaFold3 improves by 50% over best prior methods on protein-ligand binding structure prediction; covers DNA/RNA/small molecule complexes.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Not freely available for commercial drug discovery; does not model binding kinetics (only thermodynamic structures); cannot generate novel ligands.

papers.title: Interaction-constrained 3D Molecular Generation Using a Diffusion Model Enables Structure-Based Pharmacophore Modeling
papers.key_contribution: DiffPharma: conditional diffusion model with interaction-specific neural networks generates drug candidates satisfying pharmacophore constraints derived from protein-ligand structures; moves beyond AlphaFold3 structure prediction to structure-conditioned molecular generation.

idea_genome.problem_genome: AlphaFold3 predicts binding structures but cannot generate novel ligands; DiffDock generates binding poses but not molecular structures; no model combines pharmacophore-constrained generation with 3D structural awareness.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Conditional diffusion with interaction-specific neural networks that enforce pharmacophore constraints from protein-ligand structures; generates drug candidates satisfying geometric and chemical interaction requirements derived from the binding pocket.
idea_genome.observation_genome: DiffPharma generates pharmacophore-satisfying 3D molecules conditioned on protein-ligand structural information; advances structure-based drug design beyond pose prediction to active molecular generation.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Optimizes for binding geometry (pharmacophore satisfaction) without explicit ADMET property optimization; binding geometry does not guarantee in vivo efficacy; multi-objective drug design (affinity + ADMET + synthesizability) not addressed.

edges.evolution_focus: MPNN's molecular graph convolution framework radiates from isolated molecular property prediction to protein-ligand complex binding affinity prediction in 3D.
EquiBind extends AtomConv from scoring given poses to directly predicting binding geometry; same SE(3)-equivariant GNN framework, adds cross-attention for pose prediction.
DiffDock replaces EquiBind's direct regression of single pose with diffusion over the full SE(3) pose distribution; generates diverse multi-modal hypotheses — fundamentally new generative paradigm.
AlphaFold3 hybridizes DiffDock's diffusion-over-coordinates with AlphaFold2's Evoformer structure prediction into a unified model covering all biomolecular complex types.

DiffPharma radiates AlphaFold3's structure prediction framework to structure-conditioned molecular generation: leverages protein-ligand structural information not just for prediction but to actively generate pharmacophore-satisfying drug candidates.

open_question: DiffPharma generates pharmacophore-constrained molecules but optimizes only for binding geometry without explicit ADMET property optimization. What mechanism would enable joint multi-objective drug design — simultaneously satisfying pharmacophore constraints, binding affinity, ADMET properties, and synthetic accessibility within a single diffusion framework?

question.name: Question
question.description: Present only the domain/subfield and the curated May-2026 research question; ask the model to propose a next-step research direction in Computational Chemistry / Structure-Based Drug Design.

library.name: Library
library.description: Present the curated research frontier plus 6 trace papers in random order with paper titles and brief abstracts, but without explicit lineage order, genome fields, or dynamics labels; ask the model to propose the next research direction.

lineage.name: Lineage
lineage.description: Present the curated research frontier plus the ordered trace with genome metadata, GenomeDiff-style change evidence, and parent-child relationships; ask the model to predict the next evolutionary step without naming predefined category labels.

frontier_evidence.title: Interaction-constrained 3D Molecular Generation Using a Diffusion Model Enables Structure-Based Pharmacophore Modeling
frontier_evidence.unresolved_gap: Optimizes for binding geometry (pharmacophore satisfaction) without explicit ADMET property optimization; binding geometry does not guarantee in vivo efficacy; multi-objective drug design (affinity + ADMET + synthesizability) not addressed.
frontier_evidence.why_frontier: Used as the May 2026 frontier anchor: the arena question targets a limitation that remains unresolved at the endpoint of this lineage.

frontier_status.manual_review: Re-audited on 2026-05-05 for appendix release: active arena trace has a 2025/2026 frontier endpoint, a non-solved open research question, valid forward lineage edges, and generation settings aligned with post-hoc dynamics evaluation.
frontier_status.prompt_role: The same research question is shown in Question, Library, and Lineage settings; only the supporting literature/lineage context changes.
